seoTitle: How to Start Over in TurboTax Without Losing Data
metaDescription: Made an entry error and need a clean slate? Here's how the Start Over feature in TurboTax lets you reset your return the right way.
intro: Sometimes a return gets tangled up enough that starting fresh sounds easier than fixing it piece by piece. TurboTax's Start Over feature gives you a way to reset your entries without necessarily losing everything, and this guide covers when to use it and how to approach it correctly.

steps.heading: Decide if starting over is really the right move
steps.body: Consider whether your situation calls for a full reset or whether individual corrections would work just as well, since starting over is meant for more significant errors or changes.

steps.heading: Locate the Start Over feature
steps.body: Find where TurboTax houses this option within your return, since it's built specifically for resetting progress rather than editing single fields.

steps.heading: Follow the reset process step by step
steps.body: Work through TurboTax's guided steps for starting over, paying attention to what data is preserved versus what gets cleared.

steps.heading: Re-enter your information carefully
steps.body: Once reset, go back through your entries methodically to avoid repeating the same mistakes that prompted the reset in the first place.

steps.heading: Apply best practices going forward
steps.body: Use what you learned from the issue that led to starting over to keep your entries accurate for the rest of the filing process.

faq.question: Will I lose all my data if I use Start Over in TurboTax?
faq.answer: The feature is designed to let you reset entries without necessarily starting completely from scratch, though the exact scope of what's cleared depends on where you use it in your return.

faq.question: When would I need to start over in TurboTax?
faq.answer: Common scenarios include correcting significant errors or accounting for major changes in your financial situation, according to the tutorial.

faq.question: Is starting over useful for business tax filings too?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial specifically notes this is useful for businesses that need to ensure accuracy in their financial reporting, not just individual filers.

faq.question: How do I make sure I don't need to start over again?
faq.answer: Following best practices for accurate entry after your reset, and double-checking figures as you go, can help you avoid needing to repeat the process.
